g.title: cluster_rl
g.text: REINFORCEMENT LEARNING (×10 GRPO + DPO + DAgger, on contaminated bases)

g.title: cluster_inf2
g.text: INFERENCE on the CLEAN fair weights (aided)

g.title: sft_3m
g.text: writes a guess letter-by-letter; spell warm-up, then imitates a near-optimal solver

g.title: sft_5m
g.text: deeper net, deeper warm-up, more teacher data

g.title: bpe_12m
g.text: from-scratch subword tokenizer on the word list; guesses as letter-chunks

g.title: grpo_5m
g.text: RL over the train set, loose KL, to move greedy play

g.title: sft_25m_div
g.text: train on full valid-word secrets (not just answers) to stop memorizing

g.title: scale_99m
g.text: the 25M recipe scaled up, old answer-only data

g.title: self_distill_25m
g.text: SFT on its own always-valid beam+dict games

g.title: beam_dict
g.text: beam search ± constrained to real words via trie (no training)

g.title: norepeat_decode
g.text: beam(10) + dict-trie + never re-emit a prior guess

g.title: sft_deep
g.text: redesigned net on strong-teacher answers, trained to convergence; no CoT/aux

g.title: sft_aux
g.text: training-only loss pushing each letter toward real-word paths; no dict at play

g.title: cot_eph
g.text: throwaway scratchpad: think regenerated each turn, discarded at play; board-only history

g.title: sft_aux_xl
g.text: scale and aux stacked, wall-clock capped

g.title: structured_ctx
g.text: raw board vs board + explicit greens/present/absent block

g.title: rl_polish
g.text: validity+consistency reward on the 0.436 base, revert-on-regress

g.title: rl_infogain
g.text: add the information-gain reward term + 12 train guesses

g.title: passk
g.text: sample N full games/secret, count any win

g.title: cot_14m
g.text: writes a short <think> candidate list, then commits

g.title: cot_50m
g.text: scale the winning CoT with teacher reasoning traces

g.title: cot_show
g.text: A/B: teacher-context (rebuilds past think) vs self-context

g.title: rl_expert_10row
g.text: keep winning rollouts, rebuild with clean think, SFT; teach rows 7–10

g.title: dpo_commit
g.text: DPO on win/loss first-divergence commit pairs; raise P(winning commit)

g.title: dpo_decisive
g.text: clean labels: secret-commit vs wrong consistent word at the same board

g.title: constraint_aux
g.text: extra aux: keep greens / reuse yellows / no repeats

g.title: audit
g.text: Lead/Code/Telemetry/Devil's-Advocate, every claim a file:line receipt

g.title: clean_rerun
g.text: the 0.616 recipe with train-only pools, disjoint VAL/TEST

g.title: self_consistency
g.text: sample 12 traces/turn, commit the majority vote (no filter)

g.title: deployed
g.text: trained on all 2,315 known answers (fixed set = the real game)

g.title: oreo_11m
g.text: TinyStories byte-BPE pretrain, then SFT on game transcripts

g.title: rl_perguess
g.text: episode = one guess, clean per-guess credit

g.title: rl_dict
g.text: behavior policy samples trie-valid words, push free-gen toward high-adv

g.title: rl_constrained
g.text: behavior samples the still-consistent set (answer surfaced ~22%)

g.title: rl_grpo_polish
g.text: stabilized token-GRPO on the CoT policy (eval-mode forward, k3 KL)

g.title: dpo_guessonly
g.text: score preference on only the 5 committed letters

g.title: dagger_v1
g.text: relabel its bad boards with teacher's word, SFT (corrections ~1:6)

g.title: rl_grpo_reward
g.text: token-GRPO with the full updated reward (repeat/drop-present)

g.title: overnight_clean_grpo
g.text: stabilized GRPO + full reward on clean base

g.title: fair_stage1
g.text: candidate/teacher pools = full dictionary (knows spelling, not answer-hood); pretrain 30 + aux λ=1.0

g.title: distill_constrained
g.text: imitate its own dictionary-constrained rollouts (+aux), eval free-gen

g.title: infogain_xit_c
g.text: constrained rollouts (wheel), keep high-info-gain turns, SFT free-gen

g.title: dpo_fair
g.text: the contaminated-winning DPO recipe, clean base

g.title: rft_stage2
g.text: distill the model's OWN best-of-N winning games back into greedy (RAFT)

g.title: validity_max
g.text: crank aux + distill own constrained-decode games to push in-weights spelling

g.title: infill
g.text: green+yellow template as INPUT; clue-aware aux training-only. Valid opener (slate) but template-with-content breaks constrained turns
FAILED 0.05 (λ6/λ1 both) — explicit template net-negative, hurts like structured-context

g.title: fair_stage1->infill
g.text: clue-aware infill (template input, clue logic training-only)

g.title: constrained_decode
g.text: greedy masked to real-word spellings; model still deduces

g.title: bestof16
g.text: sample 16, keep real words, majority vote

g.title: beam_trie
g.text: sequence-level argmax over valid words (best deterministic)

g.title: grpo_full_fair
g.text: stabilized GRPO + full reward from the distilled base, 150 updates

g.title: control_teacher
g.text: plain gentle re-train, no special ingredients — same VAL-selection procedure
VAL 0.365 by chance → TEST 0.259 — proves the win gains are noise

g.title: validity_max_v2
g.text: push aux + more always-valid self-distill; honest clean protocol (no dict)

g.title: validity_max_v3
g.text: push aux harder still — validity lever PLATEAUS

g.title: validity_max_v4
g.text: same recipe, scaled to all 1852 train secrets — DATA lever (better deduction generalization)

g.title: dense_encode
g.text: honest port of colleague's '90%' V2: replace raw history w/ clean digest (greens-by-pos, yellows-w-excluded-pos, grays-w-exact-count) as the ONLY input; dense-format warm-up, free char-gen, train-only secrets, disjoint TEST
REJECTED 0.065 TEST — MEMORIZES (train 0.317 vs held 0.075); their 90% was contamination, not the encoding

g.title: validity_max_v4->dense_encode
g.text: honest port of mm V2 dense encoding

g.title: grpo_honest
g.text: FIRST clean-lineage RL: correct free-gen GRPO (CoT-bearing rollout, win-dominance+non-word reward, group-rel adv no/std, clipped surrogate, k3 KL to frozen ref) on validity_max_v4; fixed the dropout-in-ratio bug so RL works mechanically; train-only secrets, eval clean
NULL 0.332 TEST — train win 0.41->0.60 (+19pt) but held-out FLAT; RL sharpens/memorizes train, zero transfer (the generalization wall, now via correct RL)

g.title: reason_cot
g.text: condition on RAW history, DERIVE + write out the constraint state (greens/yellows/grays) as CoT, then guess; teacher supervises the derivation (train-only); free ephemeral decode
NULL (stopped ep20, win ~0.04) — reasoning COLLAPSED (derive-acc pinned 0.679 = all-BLANK); model routes around it. Restating a deterministic fn of the input adds NO info

g.title: ensemble
g.text: 5 trained models vote per board, majority (no dictionary) — pure multi-model test-time compute
0.283 — NULL (averages toward weaker members; below v4 0.332)

g.title: iter_refine
g.text: learned edit operator: condition on history + a DRAFT word, output a better word; at play feed each guess back K passes (full-word lookahead); lean 36-vocab; near-miss/identity/random draft training
NULL 0.098 TEST — refinement = IDENTITY (passes 0=1=3 byte-identical); model ignores the draft. Same route-around as reason-CoT

g.title: poe
g.text: fuse FROZEN generator G (validity_max_v4, common words) x a NEW consistency expert C (trained answer-agnostic on full-dict clue->consistent-word, 50k states) at the decode logits; beta sweep incl beta=0=G-alone; honest (both nets forward-pass, no dict/engine)
NULL 0.332 (delta +0.000 vs G) — C adds nothing at low beta, HURTS at high; learns 'a consistent word' but can't pin THE answer on held-out (the wall, relocated to fusion)

g.title: ambiguity_diag
g.text: measure WHY G loses: |consistent set| at each losing guess (exact full-dict scan)
KEY: wall is NOT ambiguity — 61% of losses have <=3 consistent words, 20% UNIQUELY determined yet missed; ceiling +0.41 if tight cases solved

g.title: poe->ambiguity_diag
g.text: why does G lose? measure the consistent set

g.title: poe_sharp
g.text: PoE with C retrained on TIGHT realistic states (the endgame curriculum); decisive test = can C produce the UNIQUE consistent word on held-out unique-answer states?
NULL 0.332 (delta +0.000); size-1 deduction acc 0.07 held / 0.14 train — constraint->unique-answer is a discrete SEARCH, not a learnable-generalizable fn. THE root cause of the 0.34 wall

g.title: ambiguity_diag->poe_sharp
g.text: losses are tight -> sharpen C on the endgame

g.title: format_bakeoff
g.text: fair from-scratch test of board LAYOUTS (letters-then-colors vs each letter beside its clue vs alphabet-state suffix); metric=clue-RESPECT (0.338 model violates clues 14%); full-367-TEST

NULL — interleaved respect 0.402 vs baseline 0.368 (+0.03, offset by worse spelling); early leads were learning-SPEED artifacts that converge to equal. Layout doesn't change converged tracking; deduction wall unmoved

g.title: cot_width
g.text: drift-free: force the real 0.338 model to emit N candidate-blocks before commit (N=1 less, N=10 more); also retrained K=6 A/B

NULL/NEG — more CoT MONOTONICALLY hurts (win N1 0.332 -> N3 0.264 -> N6 0.054 -> N10 0.000); less (N=1) ≈ native; retrained K=6 +0.014 sub-σ. CoT value = SINGLE draft-then-commit, not multi-candidate search. Formatting thread closed

g.title: format_bakeoff->cot_width
g.text: layout null -> does MORE CoT (search) help?
